title: Pteris platylata
intro: Pteris platylata, also known as the broad-leaved bracken fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Pteridaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its numerous health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to alleviate conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Pteris platylata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Pteris platylata is often prepared as a tea or a decoction, and is also used to make infusions, tinctures, and ointments for topical application.
taxonomy: Pteris platylata is classified as a species of fern, belonging to the family Pteridaceae. This family is commonly known as the linden-moss or wood-fern family.
